wrapUp: The last co-host is out after Belgium thrashed the United States 4-1 in Seattle, though Amadou Onana's ACL rupture leaves Rudi Garcia reshaping midfield before Friday's Los Angeles quarter-final with Spain. Argentina needed three goals in 13 minutes to survive Egypt's VAR-fuelled furore in Atlanta, while Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties in Vancouver without Johan Manzambi. France and Morocco meet in Foxborough on 9 July with Aurelien Tchouameni unlikely to return and Ismael Saibari still a game-time call, and England's Jarell Quansah suspension remains unresolved before the Miami tie with Erling Haaland's Norway. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 8 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Onana ruled out for rest of tournament with ACL rupture
stories.summary: Amadou Onana ruptured the anterior cruciate ligament in his right knee 21 minutes into Belgium's 4-1 win over the United States and will miss the quarter-final against Spain and the remainder of the World Cup. Belgium team doctor Brahim Hacene confirmed the diagnosis after scans, and BBC Sport reported Onana was seen on crutches with a knee brace, with recovery expected to take six to nine months. Hans Vanaken replaced him in Seattle and is among the options Rudi Garcia must weigh before Friday's tie in Los Angeles.

stories.headline: Tchouameni unlikely to face Morocco in Foxborough
stories.summary: Aurelien Tchouameni missed France's 1-0 win over Paraguay with a thigh and adductor problem sustained in training and France assistant Guy Stephan said on 6 July that the Real Madrid midfielder was unlikely to be fit for the 9 July quarter-final against Morocco. Get French Football News reported he had still not returned to full team training by the start of the week, with Manu Kone expected to continue alongside Adrien Rabiot while medical staff target a potential semi-final return.

stories.headline: Saibari race continues before Morocco face France
stories.summary: Ismael Saibari left Morocco's 3-0 win over Canada after 22 minutes with a thigh problem and travelled with the squad to Boston for further assessment. Reports on 7 July suggested scans showed a minor muscle strain rather than a serious tear, but Yahoo Sports said he was not pictured in training as he followed a supervised recovery programme and Mohamed Ouahbi is expected to wait for medical clearance before naming his XI. Soufiane Rahimi, who scored after coming on against Canada, is the most likely replacement if Saibari is not passed fit.

stories.headline: Manzambi misses Colombia tie and doubts Argentina quarter-final
stories.summary: Johan Manzambi sustained a knee contusion in Switzerland's final training session before the last-16 tie with Colombia and was ruled out of the goalless draw that went to penalties. AP reported the 20-year-old, who had three goals and two assists in the tournament, was one of three Swiss players to leave training early, and Murat Yakin said a full recovery before the 11 July quarter-final against Argentina was unlikely though not impossible.

stories.headline: Quansah set to miss Norway tie after Azteca dismissal
stories.summary: Jarell Quansah was sent off in the 54th minute of England's 3-2 win over Mexico after a VAR review of a high challenge on Jesus Gallardo, meaning he is suspended for the 11 July quarter-final against Norway unless FIFA intervene. BBC Sport reported the FA is considering its options after Folarin Balogun's one-match ban was suspended, deepening Thomas Tuchel's right-back crisis with Reece James still building match fitness.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Kobel saves send Switzerland past Colombia
stories.summary: Switzerland and Colombia cancelled each other out over 120 goalless minutes in Vancouver before Gregor Kobel saved Cucho Hernandez's penalty and Ruben Vargas converted the decisive kick in a 4-3 shoot-out. Murat Yakin reshuffled without Johan Manzambi, Michel Aebischer and Luca Jaquez, starting Ardon Jashari and Fabian Rieder, and Switzerland will face Argentina in Kansas City on 11 July for a first quarter-final since hosting the tournament in 1954.

stories.headline: Argentina rally from two down to reach quarter-finals
stories.summary: Egypt led 2-0 in Atlanta through Yasser Ibrahim and Mostafa Zizo, with Mostafa Shobeir also saving a Lionel Messi penalty, before Cristian Romero, Messi and Enzo Fernandez scored in the final 13 minutes to win 3-2. FIFA's match report noted Fernandez's stoppage-time header was the 3,000th goal in World Cup history, and Messi's eighth of the tournament moved him back ahead in the Golden Boot race before Argentina face Switzerland in Kansas City on 11 July.

stories.headline: De Ketelaere double drives Belgium past United States
stories.summary: Rudi Garcia again picked Charles De Ketelaere ahead of Romelu Lukaku in Seattle and was rewarded with two first-half goals plus the press that forced Matt Freese's error for Hans Vanaken's third. Sky Sports reported De Ketelaere headed in Leandro Trossard's cross two minutes after Malik Tillman's deflected free-kick had levelled the score, and Lukaku came off the bench to become the first substitute to score three goals at a single World Cup. Belgium advance to face Spain in Los Angeles on 10 July.

stories.headline: United States become final co-host eliminated
stories.summary: The United States lost 4-1 to Belgium at Lumen Field, ending Mauricio Pochettino's run at the same last-16 stage as 2014, 2022 and their previous World Cup appearance. NBC Sports reported Matt Freese's hesitation outside his box gifted Hans Vanaken a third goal and Romelu Lukaku added a stoppage-time fourth, meaning Mexico and Canada had already departed and all three co-hosts are out before the quarter-finals.

stories.headline: England's right-back options narrow before Norway
stories.summary: BBC Sport reported that Reece James has been unavailable since injuring a hamstring against Ghana and missed the Panama, DR Congo and Mexico ties, while Quansah's red card removes the defender who had started the last-16 match. Thomas Tuchel has already used James, Djed Spence, Ezri Konsa, John Stones, Quansah and Declan Rice on the right during the tournament, so the Norway selection is likely to turn on Spence or a reshaped back line.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Belgium reshape midfield with four days to Spain tie
stories.summary: Onana's ACL rupture came on his second start of the tournament and forces Rudi Garcia to reconsider the double pivot that helped Belgium overturn a 2-0 deficit against Senegal and dominate the United States. Belgium said Onana would remain with the squad in Los Angeles for the quarter-final, but ESPN noted the Red Devils must now lean on Hans Vanaken, Youri Tielemans and possibly Kevin De Bruyne without one of their most important ball-winners.

stories.headline: Egypt decry VAR decisions after Atlanta defeat
stories.summary: Hossam Hassan said Egypt were treated unfairly after VAR disallowed a Mostafa Zizo goal for a foul on Lisandro Martinez at the start of the move and Egypt felt further penalty appeals around Mohamed Salah went unchecked. BBC Sport reported a member of Egypt's backroom staff was sent off after Enzo Fernandez's winner and Hassan vowed to stop watching the remainder of the tournament, adding disciplinary and reputational noise around Argentina's quarter-final build-up.

stories.headline: Balogun reprieve raises questions over Quansah ban
stories.summary: FIFA suspended Folarin Balogun's one-match ban after his red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina, allowing the USA forward to face Belgium in Seattle, and BBC Sport reported UEFA criticised the decision as a threat to the competition's integrity. Thomas Tuchel said the precedent had created confusion over whether England could pursue a similar route for Quansah, with tournament regulations still stating a red card brings an automatic suspension for the next match.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Spain await Belgium side buoyed but depleted in midfield
stories.summary: Spain beat Portugal 1-0 in Dallas and have had extra recovery time before hosting Belgium at SoFi Stadium, while the Red Devils arrive on the back of a 4-1 Seattle win that cost them Amadou Onana. ESPN put Belgium's expected goals at 2.15 against 0.67 for the United States, but Luis de la Fuente's fifth straight clean sheet and Spain's tournament control make them favourites against a Belgium side likely to start Hans Vanaken in midfield.

stories.headline: Chalky quarter-final line-up takes shape across US
stories.summary: All eight remaining teams are ranked inside FIFA's top 20, with France, Argentina, Spain and England joined by Morocco, Belgium, Switzerland and Norway for four ties between 9 and 11 July. USA Today noted Lionel Messi leads the Golden Boot on eight goals ahead of Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland on seven and Harry Kane on six, setting up a western bracket of France-Morocco and Spain-Belgium and an eastern path through England-Norway and Argentina-Switzerland.

stories.headline: Argentina's comeback masks another defensive scare
stories.summary: Argentina have now needed extra time against Cape Verde and a three-goal burst against Egypt to survive the knockouts, with ESPN highlighting Messi's limited influence for an hour before his decisive late intervention. The holders' reward is a quarter-final with disciplined Switzerland, who kept a fifth clean sheet of the tournament against Colombia, so Lionel Scaloni still has to prove the back line can hold without another rescue act.

stories.headline: Haaland and Kane set up heavyweight Miami quarter-final
stories.summary: Norway's 2-1 win over Brazil and England's 3-2 Azteca survival mean Erling Haaland and Harry Kane, both on seven tournament goals, will meet in Miami on 11 July. Sky Sports noted Haaland's brace punished Brazil's wastefulness after Orjan Nyland's penalty save, while ESPN highlighted England as the first team ever to beat Mexico at the Azteca, giving the quarter-final a clear clash of Europe's two most decisive No 9s.
